summary: Re-review of `services/canvas-engine/src` and `test/` confirms both previously HIGH issues are fixed: `sitepull-resolver.ts` now filters captures by `metadata.target` hostname with `www.` canonicalization instead of directory-name substring matching, and `url-to-clone.ts` now releases the allocated Vite session if the post-allocation `writeFiles` seed step fails. I did not find any new regressions introduced by those patches. The three previously deferred issues are still present, so the verdict stays `warn`, but per your instruction they are not counted against the score.

issues.description: `POST /clone/stub` still streams `generateStub(body)` without a surrounding `catch`, unlike `/clone` and `/edit`. If the generator throws after headers are flushed, the connection ends in `finally` without an SSE `error` event, so clients cannot distinguish an internal failure from a clean stream completion.
issues.suggested_fix: Mirror the `/clone` and `/edit` handlers: wrap the streaming loop in `catch` and emit a terminal `{ type: 'error', ... }` SSE event before closing the response.

issues.description: The Zod anatomy schema remains permissive and can drift from the intended contract. Important fields such as `fetch.url`, `metadata.target`, and `metadata.timestamp` are accepted as arbitrary strings, and the objects are not `.strict()`, so malformed or surplus data can still pass local validation and fail later in prompt-building or resolver logic.
issues.suggested_fix: Tighten the schema to match the contract more closely: use URL/datetime validation where expected, and make the top-level/object shapes strict (or explicitly passthrough where needed) so schema acceptance matches downstream assumptions.

issues.description: The Vite pool still has the same TOCTOU port-allocation race. `allocate()` picks a port from the in-memory session map before the new server is registered, so concurrent allocators can choose the same port and then collide at bind time; the retry path only narrows the window, it does not remove it.
issues.suggested_fix: Serialize allocation or reserve the chosen port before starting Vite (for example with an in-memory reservation set/mutex), so concurrent `allocate()` calls cannot select the same candidate port.

strengths: `services/canvas-engine/src/pipeline/sitepull-resolver.ts` now uses capture metadata rather than directory naming, which closes both the false-negative and false-positive hostname matching cases from
`services/canvas-engine/src/pipeline/url-to-clone.ts` now cleans up allocated sessions when seeding fails, preventing leaked Vite servers, ports, and session directories.
I did not find any new correctness issues introduced by the two patches under review.
